title: Classifier Context Rot: Monitor Performance Degrades with Context Length
summary: Classifier Context Rot: Monitor Performance Degrades with Context Length
whyItMatters: Monitoring coding agents for dangerous behavior using language models requires classifying transcripts that often exceed 500K tokens, but prior agent monitoring benchmarks rarely contain transcripts longer than 100K tokens. We show that when used as classifiers, current frontier models fail to notice dangerous actions more often in longer transcripts. In pa…
